title: dactylorhiza lapponica subsp. angustata
intro: Dactylorhiza lapponica subsp. angustata, also known as the narrow-leaved summer orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the orchid family, native to Arctic and alpine regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its terpenoid and flavonoid compounds. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza lapponica subsp. angustata include its ability to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying and processing the aerial parts of the plant.
